task_completion.description: Verify the Days Overdue formula uses TODAY() and checks for empty Return Dates.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the formula bar when a cell in the 'Days Overdue' column (Column F) is selected. The formula must contain the 'TODAY()' function and a condition checking if the 'Return Date' cell (Column E) is empty (e.g., E2="").

task_completion.description: Verify the Days Overdue values are calculated correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at Column F (Days Overdue). It should contain numerical values. For any row where Column E (Return Date) has a date filled in, the Days Overdue must be 0.

task_completion.description: Verify the Late Fee values are calculated correctly at $1 per day.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at Column G (Late Fee). It should contain numerical or monetary values. The value in Column G must exactly match the number in Column F (since the fee is $1/day). Returned items must show 0 or $0.00.

task_completion.description: Verify the Status column correctly identifies URGENT items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at Column H (Status). It should contain text statuses. Any row where the Days Overdue (Column F) is 7 or greater MUST have the status 'URGENT'.

task_completion.description: Verify all 13 items have been processed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at rows 2 through 14 in the spreadsheet. Columns F, G, and H must be fully populated for all 13 tools with no empty cells.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used LibreOffice Calc to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The LibreOffice Calc application window should be visible in the screenshots, showing the spreadsheet interface rather than a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used spreadsheet formulas rather than manually typing answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: When a cell in Column F, G, or H is selected, the formula bar at the top should show a spreadsheet formula (e.g., starting with '=IF(' or '=') rather than a hardcoded number or text string.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a Python script in the terminal to bypass spreadsheet interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows visible where the agent wrote and executed a script (like Python or bash) to directly modify the spreadsheet file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The spreadsheet contains 13 items in rows 2-14. Columns are A-H: Item Name, Borrower, Checkout Date, Due Date, Return Date, Days Overdue, Late Fee, Status. Late fees are exactly $1.00 per day overdue. URGENT status applies to items 7 or more days overdue. Returned items (where Return Date is not empty) must show 0 days overdue and $0.00 late fee. The Days Overdue formula must use the TODAY() function and check for an empty Return Date cell (e.g., E2=""). Allow a ±1 day tolerance for date calculations.
